declared_source_plus_decoder_max_length.algorithm: Decode the declared source, then take the maximum possible copy length from that source bounded by emitted text and remaining book length.
declared_source_plus_decoder_max_length.descriptor_cost_status: Retains the full source ledger and still needs length exceptions; not a full joint derivation.

earliest_source_plus_target_max_length.algorithm: With the future target chunk known, choose the earliest source for the declared copied chunk and extend it to the maximal target match.
earliest_source_plus_target_max_length.descriptor_cost_status: High coverage but depends on future target text; retained as encoder-oracle evidence only.

previous_end_plus_decoder_max_length.algorithm: Use previous copy end as a state-only source default and take decoder max length.

unique_source_plus_decoder_max_length.algorithm: When the declared-length target chunk has only one source candidate, pair that source with decoder max length.
unique_source_plus_decoder_max_length.descriptor_cost_status: Uniqueness is evaluated against the future target chunk, so it cannot remove source during decoding.

scope.tested_question: Whether copy source and copy length become decoder-derived when evaluated as a joint dependency instead of separate ledgers.

summary.interpretation: The joint view shows that the latest source-substituted formula traded away part of the earlier all-earliest source canonicality. The remaining joint source/length regularity is still mostly encoder-side and does not turn the pair into a decoder-known rule. Source remains target-dependent, and the only decoder-valid length default after a declared source covers 60 of 261 copy events.
